title: dubautia reticulata
intro: Dubautia reticulata, also known as the Hawaiian hibiscus or Ma'o, is a species of flowering plant in the mallow family, Malvaceae, native to the Hawaiian Islands. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of wounds, skin irritations, and fever. The plant's leaves and flowers contain flavonoids and anthocyanins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Dubautia reticulata has been used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and salves, to support wound healing and promote overall well-being.
